task_completion.description: The 'web-app' Deployment has all 3 replicas running.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Rancher UI (Workloads -> Deployments) or terminal ('kubectl get deploy -n dev-environment'), the 'web-app' deployment shows 3/3 ready or active replicas.

task_completion.description: The 'db-backend' StatefulSet has its pod running.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Rancher UI (Workloads -> StatefulSets) or terminal ('kubectl get sts -n dev-environment'), the 'db-backend' StatefulSet shows 1/1 ready or active pods.

task_completion.description: The PVC for the 'db-backend' StatefulSet is bound to a locally available storage class.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Rancher UI (Storage -> PersistentVolumeClaims) or terminal ('kubectl get pvc -n dev-environment'), the PVC associated with 'db-backend' shows a status of 'Bound'.

task_completion.description: The 'data-processor' Deployment has its pod running.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Rancher UI (Workloads -> Deployments) or terminal ('kubectl get deploy -n dev-environment'), the 'data-processor' deployment shows 1/1 ready or active pods.

task_completion.description: The 'web-ingress' Ingress has successfully acquired an IP address.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Rancher UI (Service Discovery -> Ingresses) or terminal ('kubectl get ingress -n dev-environment'), the 'web-ingress' resource displays an assigned IP address under the 'Address' or 'ADDRESS' column.

integrity.description: The agent applied changes to the Kubernetes cluster rather than just editing the local manifest file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent using 'kubectl apply', 'kubectl edit', or making changes directly in the Rancher UI, rather than only modifying '/home/ga/Desktop/prod_manifests.yaml' in a text editor without applying it.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with resources in the correct 'dev-environment' namespace.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal commands explicitly include '-n dev-environment' or the Rancher UI namespace filter is set to 'dev-environment'.

integrity.description: The agent did not fake terminal outputs to simulate success.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal outputs show standard, properly formatted 'kubectl' responses, or the Rancher UI displays genuine system-generated status badges (e.g., green 'Active' indicators). No 'echo' or text-printing commands were used to fake the status.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target namespace for all resources is 'dev-environment'. Ensure all visual checks for Deployments, StatefulSets, PVCs, and Ingresses are confirmed within this specific namespace.
